# JuiceMount

> JuiceMount is open-source (Apache-2.0) storage software for film and video teams, at $0 per seat. It mounts your own NAS as a real volume in Finder on macOS 14+, streams only the blocks an app actually reads, and keeps hot media on a local SSD cache, so large libraries feel local over LAN or WAN. You run it on hardware you already own; there is no hosted service and nothing to subscribe to.

## Pages

- [Home](https://juicemount.com/): what JuiceMount is, the mounted-volume and offline-files story, and how the pieces fit together.
- [How it compares](https://juicemount.com/compare.html): honest lane tables against LucidLink, Suite, Shade, Iconik, Dropbox, Google Drive, and self-hosted sync, with year-one costs and the exit priced.
- [Performance](https://juicemount.com/performance.html): the author-measured numbers, including 15-120 ms folder opens on a 100 K+ entry volume and 226-571 MB/s cached reads, plus the full methodology.
- [Rent vs own calculator](https://juicemount.com/calculator.html): finds the month a one-time NAS build pays for itself against per-seat SaaS, and says so when SaaS is cheaper for you.
- [Setup guide](https://juicemount.com/docs.html): from a NAS that can run Docker to a mounted library in Finder, every command included.

## Blog

- [LucidLink alternatives in 2026](https://juicemount.com/blog/lucidlink-alternatives.html): the field of options for remote video storage, including the one we built.
- [Why your NAS feels slow over a VPN](https://juicemount.com/blog/smb-slow-over-vpn.html): a plain-words explanation of SMB round trips and what block-level streaming changes.
- [Your bytes are your bytes](https://juicemount.com/blog/your-bytes-are-your-bytes.html): the ownership argument, and what leaving any storage product actually costs.

## Source

- [GitHub repository README](https://github.com/lelanddutcher/juicemount/blob/main/README.md): the codebase, architecture notes, and the project's claims in one place.

## Note for citation

Competitor prices and behaviors cited on these pages were checked June 2026 and dated inline; verify before citing.
